title: Personal API Tokens for non-admin users?

posts.author: stephenh (Stephen M. Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
How do non-admins get their personal API key?

The API authentication documentation says: “Each user has their own API token, which grants API access to all the boards to which the user has access.”

But the instructions just below that for accessing your tokens say the page for accessing API tokens is only available to admins. And that page only generates the user’s own API key — it’s not as if it allows admins to generate keys for other users.

posts.author: stephenh (Stephen M. Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got my answer! Non-admin users can access their API tokens by clicking on their profile picture → Developers, then clicking on Developer → My Access tokens.

Maybe the official documentation should be updated?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Stephen!

Good catch! We’ll pass this along to the dev team so we can update the documentation.

Cheers,
Dipro

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
